creative_brief: Hero image: warm, slightly overexposed kitchen scene — a mum unpacking a brown paper bag of groceries onto a timber bench, a child reaching for an egg carton. No logos visible on products. Below-fold: mosaic of real farm photography — Rachel Ward's Kempsey property, Collins Wild Caught boats, Bundarra Farm paddocks. Earthy palette, no stock photography.
headline: "I don't have time to research every farm."
subhead: Neither did a Brookvale mum with two kids and a full-time job. She just wanted to stop guessing what was really in her fridge. Here's what changed.
hero_copy: She wasn't an activist. She wasn't even a label-reader — until her six-year-old asked why their chicken "tasted weird." That question sent her down a rabbit hole she didn't expect. Supermarket beef travelling 2,400km before it hits the shelf. Up to 21 days in cold storage before it reaches your kitchen. "Grass-fed" labels that don't mention the feedlot finishing. She didn't want a hobby. She wanted someone to have already done the work. That's when she found FarmThru.

sections.heading: She didn't change her life. She changed one habit.
sections.body: No new kitchen. No farmers' market at 6am with two kids in tow. She opened her phone on a Tuesday night, browsed regenerative beef from Rachel Ward's farm in Kempsey, NSW. Added wild-caught Alaskan salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. Pasture-raised eggs from Farmer Brown. Bio-dynamic dairy from Paris Creek Farms in South Australia. Pork belly from Bundarra Farm. She ordered in nine minutes. Collected from the Brookvale hub on Thursday after school drop-off. Days old, not weeks old — straight from their farm to our hub to her kitchen. 100% sourced from regenerative farms. Animals raised on pasture. No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. She didn't become a different person. She just stopped settling for food that had already given up before it reached her fridge.

sections.heading: The part that surprised her most
sections.body: It wasn't the taste — though her son stopped complaining about the chicken. It was how little effort it took. No subscription. No commitment. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. She expected sacrifice. She got groceries that were simply honest. Every product traces back to a named farm. Every animal raised the way her grandmother would have expected. She told us: "I didn't have time to research every farm. I just needed someone who already had." That's exactly what FarmThru does. We've built the relationships with regenerative farmers across NSW and beyond so you don't have to.

sections.heading: This is bigger than one family's groceries
sections.body: FarmThru is building a new kind of grocery store — farm-direct, regenerative, and priced for everyday families. And right now, you can be part of it. We're inviting everyday people to own a piece of FarmThru through our equity crowdfunding campaign. Not as customers. As partners. This is your chance to vote for change with your wallet — and to back a food system that puts farmers and families first. Like any early-stage investment, there are real risks involved, including the possibility of losing your investment. But if you believe grocery shopping should be transparent, regenerative, and fair — this is how you make that real. Join the waitlist. Be the first to know when the campaign goes live.

sections.heading: Start with a $5 refundable deposit
sections.body: Put down a $5 refundable deposit to join our VIP waitlist. You'll get early access to the equity crowdfunding campaign before anyone else. No obligation. Fully refundable if you change your mind. You're not committing to anything — you're just making sure you don't miss the moment.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is considering raising funds through equity crowdfunding. Any investment opportunity will be subject to applicable regulations, including the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth). Investing in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. This page is not a financial product offer. Any future offer will be made via a licensed intermediary platform and accompanied by an offer document. You should consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
